title: × Crataemespilus canescens
taxonomy: × Crataemespilus canescens is a hybrid plant species. It is classified as a hybrid of Crataegus and Mespilus genera. The classification places it within the family Rosaceae. × Crataemespilus canescens is a member of the order Rosales. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its relationships with other species in the Rosaceae family.
uses: × Crataemespilus canescens is a hybrid plant between Crataegus and Vitis, and it has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves, bark, and roots are believed to possess medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and diarrhea. The plant's leaves are also used to treat skin conditions and wounds due to their antiseptic properties. However, more studies are needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of × Crataemespilus canescens.
benefits: × Crataemespilus canescens, also known as the ash-elm, has been reported to have various health benefits in traditional medicine. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis. The plant is also thought to have antimicrobial properties, which could aid in the treatment of infections. Additionally, × Crataemespilus canescens may have antioxidant properties, helping to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. The plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, helping to combat infections and promote wound healing. constituents: × Crataemespilus canescens contains active constituents that are primarily responsible for its medicinal properties. These constituents include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. parts: × Crataemespilus canescens is a hybrid plant that is not well-documented, but based on its parent species, Crataegus and Vitis, some sources suggest that the medicinal parts of × Crataemespilus canescens may include its leaves and berries. preparations: × Crataemespilus canescens is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Crataegus monogyna and Mespilus germanica. intro: × Crataemespilus canescens is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between the boxwood (Buxus sempervirens) and the hawthorn (Crataegus spp.), characterized by its dense, thorny shrub-like appearance. The leaves of × Crataemespilus canescens have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat cardiovascular issues, such as high blood pressure and cardiac arrhythmias, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. × Crataemespilus canescens is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional European folk medicine to support heart health and reduce inflammation.
